_meta.source: Ember — China Cleantech Export Data (imports) + European Electricity Review 2026 (milestones)
_meta.note: Battery/EV import values and monthly series are from the Ember China Cleantech Export dataset. Milestones are stated in the Ember European Electricity Review 2026. EV figures shown only where the country is in the EV top-30 dataset.

countries.name: Germany
countries.headline: Solar overtook gas
countries.milestone: Solar overtook gas in 2025 — yet Germany still wasted ~9.6 TWh of wind and solar to curtailment. It co-leads the EU battery pipeline: the prime candidate for storage.

countries.name: Netherlands
countries.headline: Clean power passed fossil
countries.milestone: Wind and solar overtook fossil fuels for the first time in 2025, powered by a 31% jump in solar.

countries.name: France
countries.headline: 75+ TWh wind & solar
countries.milestone: Wind and solar generation topped 75 TWh in 2025, complementing a nuclear-heavy grid.

countries.name: Hungary
countries.headline: EU’s top solar share · 28%
countries.milestone: The highest solar share in the EU — 28% of the country’s electricity.

countries.name: Spain
countries.headline: Record 22% solar
countries.milestone: Solar hit a record 22% of power. After April’s Iberian blackout, Spain fast-tracked new rules to build batteries.

countries.name: Poland
countries.headline: Coal down to 51%
countries.milestone: Coal fell to a record-low 51% of power. Poland now co-leads the EU battery pipeline with Germany.

countries.name: Bulgaria
countries.headline: Wind & solar above 20%
countries.milestone: Wind and solar climbed above 20% — closing in on overtaking fossil fuels.

countries.name: Belgium
countries.headline: Europe’s EV gateway
countries.milestone: An exceptionally sunny start to 2025 lifted solar output 26%. Belgium is also the EU’s top gateway for Chinese EVs.

countries.name: Italy
countries.headline: EU battery leader
countries.milestone: The EU’s battery leader — about 1.9 GW, a fifth of all EU capacity, up 40% in 2025. Its batteries are starting to cover the evening gas peak.
